2604.13515.title: SFT-GRPO Data Overlap as a Post-Training Hyperparameter for Autoformalization
2604.13515.paper_summary: Supervised fine-tuning (SFT) followed by Group Relative Policy Optimization (GRPO) is a common post-training recipe. We conduct a controlled ablation over SFT-GRPO data overlap, evaluating Qwen3-8B (thinking disabled) post-trained for Lean 4 autoformalization under six conditions that differ solely in training recipe: a base model, SFT-only, GRPO-only, and three SFT+GRPO configurations where 0 percent, 30 percent, or 100 percent of the GRPO prompts coincide with the SFT corpus. Keeping SFT and GRPO data disjoint consistently outperforms full overlap at zero additional compute cost. Evaluating on Gaokao-Formal and PutnamBench under both compile pass at k and semantic pass at k assessed by an LLM judge, we find that lower overlap is monotonically associated with higher compilation and semantic accuracy. At 0 percent overlap, GRPO yields a 10.4 percentage point semantic gain over SFT alone on Gaokao, while at 100 percent overlap both metrics remain flat, rendering the GRPO stage effectively redundant. We further show that dual-metric evaluation reveals compile semantic gaps exceeding 30 percentage points for the highest compiling models, a disparity invisible under compile-only benchmarking. To our knowledge, this is the first controlled investigation of SFT-GRPO data overlap as a post-training hyperparameter, demonstrating how model behavior varies based on the degree of data sharing between training stages.
2604.13515.paper_summary_zh: 監督微調（SFT）隨後進行的群體相對策略優化（GRPO）是一種常見的後訓練方法。我們對SFT-GRPO數據重疊進行了控制性消融，評估了在六種僅在訓練配方上有所不同的條件下進行的Qwen3-8B（思考功能禁用）後訓練，用於Lean 4自動形式化：一個基礎模型、僅SFT、僅GRPO，以及三種SFT+GRPO配置，其中0％、30％或100％的GRPO提示與SFT語料庫重疊。保持SFT和GRPO數據不重疊在零額外計算成本的情況下始終優於完全重疊。在Gaokao-Formal和PutnamBench上進行評估，根據LLM評判者在k的編譯通過和k的語義通過，我們發現較低的重疊與更高的編譯和語義準確性單調相關。在0％重疊時，GRPO在Gaokao上比僅SFT提高了10.4個百分點的語義增益，而在100％重疊時，兩個指標保持平穩，使得GRPO階段實際上變得多餘。我們進一步顯示，雙指標評估揭示了對於最高編譯模型，編譯語義差距超過30個百分點，這一差異在僅編譯基準下是不可見的。據我們所知，這是首次對SFT-GRPO數據重疊作為後訓練超參數進行的控制性研究，展示了模型行為如何根據訓練階段之間數據共享的程度而變化。
2604.13515.author: Xiaole Su et.al.
2604.13515.authors: Xiaole Su, Kasey Zhang, Andy Lyu
